name: Flying
notes: Flying = MOVING and REVERSING. rel_speed (rolling speed / frame background) is dimensionless, so one amplitude threshold holds across bright and backlit tubes -- it separates moving from still. direction_oscillation (lag-1 autocorrelation of flow direction) is negative when a block's motion reverses frame to frame, which is the wingbeat's temporal signature and is what separates flapping wings from a translating leg. Coherence is NOT used: at this 4px block size it pins at ~1 for everything, because the wing's spatial cancellation happens over a larger scale than one block -- the cancellation this footage exposes is temporal, hence direction_oscillation. Retune both lines on the plots; expect rel_speed in low single digits and direction_oscillation around -0.15 to -0.3 for flight.
